rulings.Case Title: ELIZABETH WRIGHT VS. AMANDA NOWINSKI ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Discovery Objections
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, April 30, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT AMANDA NOWINSKI's Motion For Relief From Waiver Of Discovery Objections Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant Amanda Nowinski's Motion for Relief From Waiver of Discovery Objections is unopposed and granted. The defendant has complied with the statutory requirements for granting said relief and good cause shown. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@sbslawsf.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants' Motion for Relief From Waiver of Objections to written discovery served on January 15, 2019 is granted. The evidence the envelopes containing the discovery requests were placed by office staff out of defendants' sight in a desk drawer in the context and point of time the corporate defendant was unrepresented and the individual defendant was in pro per is sufficient to support the relief requested. Subsequently retained counsel thereafter substantially responded to the discovery thereby meeting the applicable statutory requirements. If requested by the plaintiff at the hearing the Court will set a shortened briefing schedule and hearing date for rulings on objections interposed in the served responses. rulings.Case Title: CHARLES PITTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2019, Line 7, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's Motion To Dismiss Plaintiff'S Complaint. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to dismiss plaintiff's complaint will be granted if Plaintiff does not properly file and serve a First Amended Complaint. On May 10, 2018, the Court sustained the City's demurrer to Plaintiff's complaint, with leave to file a First Amended Complaint by July 10, 2018. Plaintiff asserts in his late-filed opposition that the amended complaint was filed with the court in a timely manner. It was not. On July 10, 2018, Plaintiff filed a "Declaration," which attached a proposed "First Amended Complaint." However, as the City's counsel twice informed Plaintiff in writing, that Declaration does not constitute a first amended complaint, which must be separately filed with the Court. Plaintiff has leave until May 10, 2019, to file and serve his first amended complaint. If Plaintiff fails to do so, the Court will grant the City's motion to dismiss the action with prejudice without the need for further hearing or briefing. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for the City is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: WALTER CRUZ VS. SILVER ARROWS REMODEL & RESTORATION INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2019, Line 13, PLAINTIFF WALTER CRUZ's DEMURRER. Plaintiff's demurrer to Defendants Silver Arrows Remodel & Restoration Inc. and Jaime Melo's (erroneously sued as Jamie Melo) answer is overruled as to defenses one and two (comparative fault defenses) because they may turn on factual issues that cannot be decided on a demurrer. The primary function of the answer is to give the other party notice so that it may prepare its case. (Harris v. City of Santa Monica (2013) 56 Cal.4th 203, 240.) A purported defect in a pleading that otherwise gives notice to the other side cannot be said to affect substantial rights or cause prejudice. (Id.) If, after a reasonable opportunity for discovery and factual investigation, Plaintiff contends that Defendants lack factual support for some or all of the affirmative defenses, Plaintiff should request that Defendants withdraw those affirmative defenses. If Plaintiff is dissatisfied by the response, then he may bring a summary adjudication motion seeking to eliminate the affirmative defenses lacking factual support. The demurrers to defenses three and five (statute of limitations defenses) are sustained without leave to amend as conceded. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: MARK R ROBERTS VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, April 30, 2019, Line 9, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Defendant City and County of San Francisco's demurrer to complaint is off calendar as moot because on April 15, 2019, Plaintiff filed a first amended complaint. The filing of the amended complaint complied with Code Civ. Proc. § 472(a) ("A party may amend its pleading once without leave of the court at any time before the answer, demurrer, or motion to strike is filed, or after a demurrer or motion to strike is filed but before the demurrer or motion to strike is heard if the amended pleading is filed and served no later than the date for filing an opposition to the demurrer or motion to strike"). In this case, while Plaintiff originally filed an opposition to the demurrer, he subsequently filed and served a timely amended complaint. Pursuant to Code Civ. Proc. 1005(b), Plaintiff's opposition was due on April 17, 2019. Plaintiff served the amended complaint on April 9, 2019 and filed it on April 15, 2019. Defendant is free to demur to the amended complaint if appropriate. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that it, he or she will not appear at the hearing. =(302/EPS)

The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
